guide.action_std_concept_desc: Ensures consistent visual feedback and property naming conventions across different components to improve development efficiency and usability.
guide.action_std_desc: Defines common rules for components that trigger user actions, such as buttons and links.
guide.action_std_note_desc: In specific contexts (e.g., Floating Action Buttons), fixed sizes other than the standard ones may be used.
guide.action_std_rule_1_desc: Manage dimensions using `sm`, `md`, and `lg` (prop name is `size`).
guide.action_std_rule_2_desc: Use Primary (High), Secondary (Medium), and Tertiary/Ghost (Low) for visual weight.
guide.action_std_rule_3_desc: Display a spinner during processing and disable interaction to prevent double submissions.
guide.action_std_rule_4_desc: Always provide an `aria-label` for icon-only buttons to support screen readers.
guide.feedback_std_concept_desc: Unifies colors and icons according to visual intent (Intent) to ensure users can instantly understand the importance of information.
guide.feedback_std_desc: Defines common rules for components that communicate system information to users, such as alerts and toasts.
guide.feedback_std_note_desc: Choose between Toast (temporary) and Alert (persistent) based on the message length and urgency.
guide.feedback_std_rule_2_desc: Uses the same icons for the same variants across all feedback components.
guide.feedback_std_rule_3_desc: Standardizes property names for titles (`title`) and descriptions (`description`).
guide.feedback_std_rule_4_desc: Provides an `onClose` property to allow users to explicitly close unnecessary information.
guide.nav_std_concept_desc: Unifies the visual representation of active states, allowing users to intuitively grasp their current location.
guide.nav_std_desc: Defines common rules for components that assist user movement and switching, such as tabs and pagination.
guide.nav_std_note_desc: Use 'Tabs' for switching major features, 'SegmentedControl' for auxiliary choices, and 'Pagination' for page navigation.
guide.nav_std_rule_1: Active Indication: Use a sliding indicator (moving bar) to achieve smooth state transitions.
guide.nav_std_rule_1_desc: Use sliding indicators (moving bars) for smooth state transitions.
guide.nav_std_rule_2: Keyboard Navigation: Supports item movement with arrow keys to ensure accessibility.
guide.nav_std_rule_2_desc: Support item movement via arrow keys to ensure accessibility.
guide.nav_std_rule_3: Responsive Support: Properly handles scrolling or collapsing based on screen width to maintain usability.
guide.nav_std_rule_3_desc: Appropriately handle scrolling or folding based on screen width to maintain usability.
guide.nav_std_rule_4: Consistent Interaction: Unifies feedback for hovers and clicks across all navigation elements.
guide.nav_std_rule_4_desc: Standardize hover and click feedback across all navigation elements.
guide.overlay_std_concept_desc: Provides a UI that allows users to complete tasks without hesitation by choosing the appropriate layering method according to information priority and contextual continuity.
guide.overlay_std_desc: Defines common rules for components displayed on top of the current context, such as dialogs and drawers.
guide.overlay_std_note_desc: Use 'Dialog' for full-screen focus, 'Popover' for supplementary information, and 'BottomSheet' for mobile selection.
guide.overlay_std_rule_1_desc: Divided into parts like `Root`, `Trigger`, and `Content` for flexible layout and logical structure.
guide.overlay_std_rule_2_desc: Declare and control open/close states using the `open` and `onOpenChange` properties.
guide.overlay_std_rule_3_desc: Ensure consistency in behaviors such as closing on background click or Escape key, focus trapping, and preventing background scrolling.
guide.overlay_std_rule_4_desc: Comply with the WAI-ARIA Modal pattern, using appropriate roles (`dialog`) and labeling (`aria-labelledby`).

button.consistency_desc: Always use the same priority level for the same type of action.
button.copy_usage: Best for accurately saving strings like IDs or URLs to the clipboard with one click.
button.group_usage: Best for presenting related operations together, such as 'Back/Next' or 'Edit/Save'.
button.guide_desc: Buttons that prompt user action should use visual weighting according to their importance and role.
button.icon_usage: Best for toolbars or cards where space is limited and intuitive operation is desired.
button.placement_conflict_desc: Keep only one Primary button per screen (or modal) in principle.
button.placement_desc: Place primary actions at the end of the common eye-tracking path (e.g., bottom right).
button.priority_danger_usage: Destructive actions that cannot be undone after execution, such as delete or reset.
button.priority_high_usage: The most important 'Primary Action' on the screen. Ideally only one per screen.
button.priority_low_usage: For supplementary operations or when many options need to be listed.
button.priority_medium_usage: Secondary actions that support the primary one. (Cancel, Edit, Back, etc.)
button.priority_weighting: When multiple buttons are on the same screen, use visual weighting to prevent user confusion.
button.semantics_desc: To maintain semantics and accessibility, choose buttons based on 'purpose' rather than just appearance.
button.semantics_guide_ref: For detailed criteria, please refer to the specific guide.
button.standard_usage: Most versatile action. Best when you need to convey intent clearly with text.
